name: crackle

documentation.readme: crackle cracks BLE Encryption (AKA Bluetooth Smart).
examples: crackle cracks BLE Encryption (AKA Bluetooth Smart).
crackle was written by Mike Ryan <mikeryan@lacklustre.net>
documentation.how_to_use: Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `crackle cracks BLE Encryption (AKA Bluetooth Smart).`. Further invocations from upstream: `crackle was written by Mike Ryan <mikeryan@lacklustre.net>`; `crackle -i input.pcap -o decrypted.pcap`.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [ble_4_x, ble_5_x, ble_mesh] in the Bluetooth category. Fits when the engagement needs: ![crackle](https://raw.github.com/mikeryan/crackle/logo/crackle.png "crackle").
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: crackle cracks BLE Encryption (AKA Bluetooth Smart).

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases: When you need Bluetooth, use crackle because ![crackle](https://raw.github.com/mikeryan/crackle/logo/crackle.png "crackle").
When you need a concrete invocation, run `crackle cracks BLE Encryption (AKA Bluetooth Smart).` as documented upstream for crackle.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `crackle was written by Mike Ryan <mikeryan@lacklustre.net>` as documented upstream for crackle.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `crackle -i input.pcap -o decrypted.pcap` as documented upstream for crackle.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `crackle -i encrypted.pcap -o decrypted.pcap -l 81b06facd90fe7a6e9bbd9cee59736a7` as documented upstream for crackle.
When chaining Bluetooth tools, pair crackle with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `crackle --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
command_examples: crackle cracks BLE Encryption (AKA Bluetooth Smart).
crackle was written by Mike Ryan <mikeryan@lacklustre.net>
